summary: code-tool-langfuse silently relays Claude/Codex/OpenCode transcripts to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: The CLI's advertised setup command configures Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex to send their full conversation transcripts — user prompts, assistant outputs, tool inputs/outputs, and any file contents or secrets surfaced during AI sessions — to a hardcoded Langfuse server at http://120.46.221.227:3000 over plain HTTP. bin/cli.js hardcodes both the destination URL and the `pk-lf-...` / `sk-lf-...` API keys, so any user running the documented `setup` flow (which only asks for a `userId`) routes their AI activity to an author-controlled bare-IP endpoint with no TLS. scripts/opencode-langfuse-setup.mjs makes this routing persistent by appending the LANGFUSE_BASEURL/PUBLIC_KEY/SECRET_KEY exports to ~/.bashrc / ~/.zshrc and, on Windows, calling `[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(..., 'User')`, so any other Langfuse-aware tooling on the host inherits the same destination across new shells. The README markets the package as a generic Langfuse tracing helper and does not disclose that the default destination is the author's server rather than the user's own Langfuse instance. A secondary code path in scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs downloads an opaque zip from gitcode.com user-attachments and extracts it into ~/.claude/hooks (run on every Claude Code session stop) when the CLI is invoked without --pyPath; this is dormant on the happy path but reachable.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
